title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 20th February 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: Accumulate from current levels for the long term; can give decent returns.
recommendations.detail: Caller asked about entering at the current price point. Kutumba Rao said buy/accumulate from current levels for the long term; can give decent returns. By long term he means hold a minimum of more than one year (don't ask back in three or six months).

recommendations.note: Buy/accumulate from current levels for the long term (>1 year hold).
recommendations.detail: Same caller query. He said both stocks (Sezzle India and Heritage Foods) should be bought/accumulated from current levels for the long term, holding a minimum of more than one year; they can give decent returns.

recommendations.note: Good dividend-yield bet as an FD alternative; have at least 3-5 years.
recommendations.detail: As an alternative to a fixed deposit, he flagged PSU dividend-yield companies and said NTPC would be a good bet (check the dividend record date before buying). He cautioned to have at least three to five years, since PSU companies can post loss-years and an ill-timed exit forces a booked loss that eats the dividend.

recommendations.note: Good dividend-yield bet as an FD alternative, alongside NTPC.
recommendations.detail: In the same FD-alternative / dividend-yield discussion he said PFC too would be a good bet. Check the dividend record date and hold with a 3-5 year horizon.

recommendations.note: Large dividend payer; PSU banks slightly high now, so add on dips.
recommendations.detail: He said banks are slightly on the higher side as of now; if they fall a bit, large dividend payers like Canara Bank can be attempted on the dip for dividend yield above the FD level.

recommendations.note: Large dividend payer; add on a pullback for dividend yield.
recommendations.detail: Mentioned alongside Canara Bank as a large dividend payer. With PSU banks slightly high now, attempt on a fall for dividend yield above the FD level.

recommendations.note: Good dividend but runs at the promoter's whim; avoid for peace of mind.
recommendations.detail: He said Vedanta gives a good dividend now, but the company runs at the promoter's whim, so leave such risky bets aside; for peace of mind the PSU dividend stocks are a better opportunity.

recommendations.note: Contrarian buy; good management, fundamentals and nationwide franchise.
recommendations.detail: For a caller seeking a good private bank (already holding RBL and Federal), Kutumba Rao recommended Kotak contrarian-wise. Good management moving ahead with good fundamentals, a strong nationwide network and an excellent franchise.

recommendations.note: Good niche engineering company; slow and steady, can go back up to 450-500. Hold.
recommendations.detail: Caller holds 5,000 shares around 550. A good company with good market share on the engineering side, pumps for a niche segment. No immediate jump expected, but slow and steady it can go back up to 450-500 easily, so hold, no worry. When buying such large quantity, buy in tranches.

recommendations.note: Don't chase; sustains only with follow-up buying. Mandatory stop-loss above 600.
recommendations.detail: Caller bought around 600 in a hurry. Buying it repeatedly is unnecessary; it made a large move (crossed 600, up to ~620) but only sustains with follow-up buying, and fell sharply after results (bottomed ~475 over three months). If buying above 600, definitely keep a trading stop-loss; entering at higher levels is risky. The Thursday spike likely came on an unconfirmed social-media trigger.
